channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record by scoring his 895th
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Ovechkin fired just about the perfect shot from the place on the ice that has defined his remarkable career. When the puck hit the net, it made him the top goal scorer in NHL history.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UH Basketball Alum and Savoy owner hosts ‘Paint the Third Ward Red’ watch party for NCAA Championship
item.description: Co-owned by University of Houston women’s basketball alum Claire “Long” Watson and Black Watson, The Savoy is set to host a no entry fee watch party, inviting all University of Houston fans to wear their Coogs gear tonight for the ‘Paint the Third Ward Red’ watch party, celebrating the Cougars’ firs
item.dc:creator: Kendall Mayes, KPRC 2

item.title: Meghan McCain makes stance on trans athletes in women's sports clear after Andy Cohen spat
item.description: Meghan McCain wrote in a column last week that girls' and women's sports should be kept female and that the issue is really about "fairness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Three’s Company: Detroit Pistons guard Malik Beasley joins elite group during Grizzlies matchup
item.description: Detroit Pistons guard Malik Beasley joined elite company during the team’s game against the Memphis Grizzlies.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Guerrero, Blue Jays agree to a $500 million, 14-year deal that starts in 2026, AP source says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Vladimir Guerrero Jr. and the Toronto Blue Jays agreed to a $500 million, 14-year contract that starts in 2026, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press, a deal that removes what would have been the biggest star from next offseason’s free-agent market.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Great starting pitching stretches not enough for the White Sox and Reds early this season
item.description: The Cincinnati Reds and Chicago White Sox both got brilliant stretches from their starting rotations — and largely wasted them.
item.dc:creator: Noah Trister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaking Wayne Gretzky's record brings joy to hockey and beyond sports
item.description: Alex Ovechkin's pursuit of Wayne Gretzky's NHL goals record became the talk of the league a time of the season usually reserved only for playoff races.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Women's college basketball looking for next star with no Paige Bueckers and JuJu Watkins injured
item.description: Women’s college basketball has been on the rise these last few years, riding the wave of iconic players like Caitlin Clark, Angel Reese, Paige Bueckers and JuJu Watkins.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TROTTO Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sagstrom beats Coughlin 1-up in T-Mobile Match Play to end 5-year drought on LPGA Tour
item.description: NORTH LAS VEGAS, Nev. (AP) — The five-year wait to end her LPGA Tour victory drought appeared to be a matter of time when Madelene Sagstrom went 4-up just six holes into the final of the T-Mobile Match Play. Then it nearly got away from her, and Sagstrom even briefly fell behind at one point.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Doncic scores 30 points as Lakers dominate NBA-leading Thunder 126-99
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Luka Doncic scored 30 points and the Los Angeles Lakers routed the league-leading Oklahoma City Thunder 126-99 on Sunday. Austin Reaves scored 20 points and LeBron James added 19 points and seven assists for the Lakers, who have won four of five.
item.dc:creator: CLIFF BRUNT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paige Spiranac's shirt sends clear message after she fires back at critic
item.description: Golf influencer Paige Spiranac had a clear message on her shirt as she FaceTimed a friend on Friday. Spiranac had a spat with a critic earlier last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Duke's push for 6th NCAA title falls apart in unlikely late collapse against Houston in Final Four
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Up by double figures in the second half at the Final Four, closing in on the chance to play for yet another national championship, Duke was right where it had worked all season to be. What happened next was unforgettable, history of the most painful kind.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Carpenter and Torkelson homer as Tigers beat White Sox 7-2 and drop Chicago to 2-6
item.description: Kerry Carpenter homered for the third time in two games and Spencer Torkelson had a home run and a double Saturday as the Detroit Tigers beat the Chicago White Sox 7-2 for their fourth win in five games following an opening three-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Strong, Fudd help UConn blow out UCLA, 85-51, in Final Four as Paige Bueckers moves 1 win from elusive title
item.description: Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd didn’t even need much help from superstar Paige Bueckers to propel UConn into the national title game, leading the Huskies to a remarkably easy 85-51 victory over UCLA on Friday night in the Final Four of the women’s NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, WGN 9

item.title: Teaching Chicago's hard-working values, basketball legend Tom Kleinschmidt builds a dynasty at DePaul Prep
item.description: DePaul College Prep's boys' basketball team, coached by Tom Kleinschmidt, has won three consecutive IHSA championships, thanks to his hard work, discipline, and respect, which he learned growing up on the city's Northwest Side.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: Caleb Williams' development, Bears search for "weapons" and Kyler Gordon's contract extension with The Athletic's Kevin Fishbain
item.description: The Chicago Bears are looking to develop Caleb Williams and use their top draft picks to bolster their offense, while also focusing on adding a dynamic running back and working on a contract extension for Kyler Gordon.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: A's routed by Cubs 18-3 in Sacramento home opener
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) -- Carson Kelly hit for the cycle and drove in five runs, Michael Busch launched a two-run homer on the way to a career-high four RBIs, and the Chicago Cubs pounded the Athletics 18-3 on Monday night to spoil their first game in their new Northern California home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 5 players, 2 coaches ejected after Pistons-Timberwolves skirmish in 2nd quarter
item.description: Five players and two coaches were ejected after a fight broke out in the second quarter in the Minnesota Timberwolves' victory over the Detroit Pistons on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK DONNELLY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amalie Arena transforms for NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: Amalie Arena is all about basketball for the next week, as the NCAA Women's Final Four takes over Tampa with events around the city.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC
